en: Get down, check it out.
B-O-M-B, bomb. T-O-M-B, tom, no, tomb.
If T-O-M-B is tomb, that's boom-da-bombs over.
But no, that's bomb, that's tomb.
Get ready, is this calm or coom?
No, that's comb. And that's why spelling bees are only won
by introverted little ugly girls.
Because they're the only ones that got enough time to memorize this.
C-O-M-B, comb. P-O-M-B, poem.
No, poem is P-O with a E with a M.
Now, P-O-E-M, poem, H-O-E-M, home.
No, home is H-O with a M with a E.
H-O-M-E, home, S-O-M-E, some.
No, that's some.
S-O-M-E, some, N-O-M-E, numb.
No, that's N-U-M-B.
The whole doggone thing is dumb.
I was always getting in trouble in school for acting smart.
Because I'd ask questions. What makes a Teflon stick to the pan?
If yogurt goes bad, how can you tell?
Where do contractors go right after they get your job half done?
If a duck eats and goes in the water, does he get the cramps?
If your knees bent the other way, what would a chair look like?
Once I got an F on the question that asked my opinion,
I got my opinion wrong.
Well, we need to teach kids to think so they can have their own opinion.
A lot of these modern questions don't have an answer.
When are you alive? When are you dead?
How much can you dance in the end zone?
This is what makes us different than the Japanese.
You see, the Japanese, they're not quite like us Americans.
See, the Japanese are driven. But us Americans, we're the drivers.
